summary: Iteration 140: Bug Fix - Edit button (pencil) for participants on appointment detail page. The original fix at line 903 was correct (changing condition from isOrganizer to isOrganizer || viewerCanPropose), but viewerCanPropose was always false because viewerParticipant lookup used user_id which was null for participants. Fixed by using viewerParticipantStatus from appointment response directly instead of looking up participant by user_id.

minor.issue: Returns 403 'Vous n'êtes pas participant de ce rendez-vous' when participant's user_id is null in DB. Backend checks user_id match but participant record may not have user_id linked when invitation was accepted.
minor.note: This is a data linkage issue - participant records should have user_id set when user accepts invitation while logged in

frontend_tests.test_1_organizer_edit_button_visible: PASS - Edit button (pencil) visible for organizer
frontend_tests.test_1_organizer_modal_opens: PASS - EditProposalModal opens when organizer clicks edit button
frontend_tests.test_1_organizer_modal_title: PASS - Modal title is 'Proposer une modification' (because there are accepted participants)
frontend_tests.test_2_accepted_participant_edit_button_: PASS - Edit button (pencil) visible for accepted participant (BUG FIXED)
frontend_tests.test_2_accepted_participant_modal_opens: PASS - EditProposalModal opens when accepted participant clicks edit button
frontend_tests.test_2_accepted_participant_modal_title: PASS - Modal title is 'Proposer une modification' for participant
frontend_tests.test_3_invited_participant_no_edit_butto: PASS - Edit button NOT visible for invited (non-accepted) participant
frontend_tests.test_4_regression_organizer_unchanged: PASS - Organizer behavior unchanged after fix

action_items: Backend: Consider linking participant user_id when user accepts invitation while logged in (POST /api/invitations/{token}/respond should set user_id if user is authenticated)
critical_code_review_comments: Original fix at line 903 was correct: changed {isOrganizer && ...} to {(isOrganizer || viewerCanPropose) && ...}
Root cause: viewerCanPropose was always false because line 698 looked up viewerParticipant by user_id, but participant record had user_id=null
Fix applied: Changed viewerCanPropose to use viewerParticipantStatus from appointment response directly (line 704-705)
Also improved viewerParticipant lookup to use invitation_token as fallback (line 699-701)
seed_data_creation: Created appointment TEST_EditButton_Participant (baa9d4bd-c92d-4a8b-9490-37394451daf6) with igaal.hanouna@gmail.com as accepted participant
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified: Edit button now works for accepted participants. The fix was in AppointmentDetail.js lines 697-705 - changed viewerCanPropose to use viewerParticipantStatus from appointment response instead of looking up participant by user_id. Test appointment: baa9d4bd-c92d-4a8b-9490-37394451daf6. Note: Backend POST /api/modifications/ may still fail for participants whose user_id is not linked in DB.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The original fix at line 903 was correct (adding viewerCanPropose to the condition), but viewerCanPropose was always false because the viewerParticipant lookup at line 698 used user_id matching, and the participant record had user_id=null (not linked to user account)
reproduction_steps: 2. Navigate to appointment where user is accepted participant
3. Edit button (pencil) was not visible because viewerCanPropose was false
4. viewerCanPropose was false because viewerParticipant was undefined
5. viewerParticipant was undefined because participants.find(p => p.user_id === user?.user_id) returned nothing (user_id was null in participant record)
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Changed viewerCanPropose to use viewerParticipantStatus from appointment response directly, which is always accurate regardless of user_id linkage
